response.justification: Candidate is a skilled full-stack software developer with strong web, backend, and some embedded experience, but the resume shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep-learning workload optimization, GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture, or hardware-software co-design. The role is a Principal Performance Architect for AI accelerator hardware, which requires deep specialization the candidate does not demonstrate. Educational background (BS in CS) without an advanced degree also falls short of the preferred PhD/MS path for this hardware-architecture-focused principal role.
strengths: Strong low-latency systems experience with DLang at Sociomantic handling millions of req/sec
Some C/C++ experience at National Instruments on engineering measurement tooling
13+ years of professional software engineering experience across diverse stacks
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator hardware experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or hardware-software co-design background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack/product engineering, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing AI accelerator / SoC modeling expertise
No GPU / SIMD-MIMD / DNN optimization background
Low-latency systems and some C/C++ exposure
